questions.prompt: Which of the following best captures cognitive control as described in lecture?
options: A set of reflexes that bypass planning
A set of processes that supports planning and flexible goal-directed behaviour
A memory store for facts and events
A sensory filter devoted only to visual attention

questions.prompt: In Shallice’s framework, contention scheduling is most closely associated with:
options: Automatic activation of action scripts by stimuli

questions.prompt: The supervisory attentional system is especially needed when a person is:
options: Facing a novel situation that requires flexibility
Performing a purely sensory discrimination with no rule change

questions.prompt: A patient with frontal lobe damage can complete routine tasks but struggles in new situations, repeats actions, and shows environmental dependency. This pattern most strongly suggests impairment of the:

Initiation refers to:
options: Reflecting on whether a just-completed response was correct
The will to start a behaviour
Switching between rules after negative feedback

questions.prompt: Difficulty both starting actions and ceasing repetitive behaviours after frontal damage was referred to as:

options: Reward magnitude and delay discounting only

options: A single storage bin for all past experiences
A group of mechanisms by which experience changes brains and behaviour
A process limited to conscious recall

questions.prompt: Which example most clearly reflects non-declarative memory?
options: Remembering what you ate this morning
Recalling where you spent last summer

options: Visuospatial sketchpad for visual/spatial information; phonological loop for auditory/phonetic information
Phonological loop for spatial maps; visuospatial sketchpad for emotional states
Central executive for all storage; hippocampus for all manipulation

options: Emotion is purely bodily physiology with no cognitive role
Emotion is subjective, physiological, displayed behaviourally, interacts with cognition, and is social
Emotion exists independently of culture and communication

Migration of neurons along radial glia

options: Earlier-born neurons settle furthest from the ventricles and youngest cells occupy deeper layers
Each wave of neurons migrates farther from the ventricles, so younger cells end up in more outer layers

options: Small reduction in synapses from the start
Massive roughly ten-fold increase in synapse number
